journal_entry.description: Purchase of land and building for warehouse, partial payment
journal_entry.rationale: Under IFRS, when land and building are purchased together, the cost must be allocated based on their relative fair values. Total fair value = $8,000,000 (land) + $4,000,000 (building) = $12,000,000. Land receives 8/12 = 66.67% and building receives 4/12 = 33.33% of the $9,000,000 purchase price. Land = $6,000,000, Building = $3,000,000. Payment: 50% ($4,500,000) is due in 6 months (accounts payable), and 50% ($4,500,000) plus 10% HST on that portion ($450,000) = $4,950,000 was paid by cheque. Note: In Ontario, commercial real estate transactions may be subject to HST if the vendor is registered and the property is commercial. The HST paid is recoverable (HST Receivable).

output_debit_classifier.reason: Purchase of land and building allocated by relative fair values per IFRS. 50% paid now with HST, 50% payable in 6 months. Land (asset increase) + Building (asset increase) debited; Accounts Payable (liability increase) and Cash (asset decrease) credited. HST on the paid portion is recoverable.

output_credit_classifier.reason: Purchase of land and building allocated by relative fair values per IFRS. 50% paid now with HST, 50% payable in 6 months. Land (asset increase) + Building (asset increase) debited; Accounts Payable (liability increase) and Cash (asset decrease) credited. HST on the paid portion is recoverable.
